description: Grappling position/chain memory for video placement suggestions. Updated from real placements and Aaron's corrections.

locked_rules.knee_on_belly: sub-position under Side Control, permanent hold
locked_rules.submissions_heading: flat named finishes (arm bar, triangle, kimura, etc.)
locked_rules.offensive_transitions: clean graph edges only, arrow format
locked_rules.folder_naming: key branch points only (sub-position, approach, finish), not every spoken micro-step

failed_submission_chains.rule: Failed submissions leading to transitions stay under submissions, chain continues

counters_go_under_opposite_perspective.rule: 'Counter to X' means the other perspective's defence. Dogfight counter = passer/defence, not guard-player/offence

direction_matters_in_naming.rule: The folder name should reflect what leads to what, not where you end up

outcome_folders_usually_unnecessary.rule: Final destination positions as leaf folders are usually unnecessary — the technique name is enough

defender_defence_vs_offence.rule: Defender/defence = surviving or neutralizing the attack. Defender/offence = actively attacking to escape or reverse. 'I defend with a whizzer' = defence. 'I escape with a knee-tap fireman' = offence.

examples: shin-whizzer: attacker/offence (countering opponent's whizzer) vs defender/defence (using your own whizzer)
limp-arm: attacker/offence (countering opponent's whizzer with limp arm) vs defender context
same_technique_both_perspectives.rule: The same technique name can appear under both perspectives. The perspective determines WHOSE action it is: 'they whizzer, I counter' = attacker/offence. 'I whizzer' = defender/defence.

examples: attacker = the person shooting (on the single leg)
defender = the person being shot on (defending the takedown)
shot_perspective_key.rule: In shots: attacker = shooter, defender = person being shot on. 'I shoot single leg, they whizzer, I limp arm' = attacker/offence. 'They shoot, I whizzer' = defender/defence.

known_chains.sequence: Front Headlock > Head in the hole go-behind > Whip by
Front Headlock > Pinch front headlock to anaconda
Mount > S mount > Arm bar
Mount > Arm triangle > Opponent turtles > Reverse D'Arce
Mount > S mount > Mounted triangle
North South > Top side crucifix > Kimura
Turtle > Seatbelt > Rolling back take
Half butterfly > Saddle entry counter > North south pass
J point > North south pass > Reverse collar tie
J point > Toreando pass
Knee shield half guard > (counter) Wrestle up > Dogfight > Bodylock roll
known_chains.note: Passer perspective because it is a counter to guard player's wrestle-up

known_chains.sequence: Knee shield half guard > Backstep > North south pass
Knee shield half guard > Over-under pass > Smash pass > J point > Toreando
Knee shield half guard > Quarter guard > Backstep saddle > Inside ashi > Outside heel hook
Seated Guard > Snap to front headlock
Supine Guard > J point > Bodylock > Half guard entry
Supine Guard > J point > Leg drag
Supine Guard > Single under pass
2-on-1 > Throw by > Rear bodylock
Collar tie > Feint snap > Front headlock
Collar tie > Throw by to 2-on-1 > Ankle pick
Underhook > Snap down > Front headlock
Wrist control > Arm drag > Takedown
Single leg > Shin whizzer defence > Running the pipe
Front Headlock (defender) > Wrist control spin out
Front Headlock (defender) > Knee-tap fireman
known_chains.note: Offence = attacking escape (you attack to get out)

known_chains.sequence: Single leg (attacker) > Shin whizzer > Limp arm counter
known_chains.note: Attacker = shooter. They whizzer, I limp arm to counter.

known_chains.sequence: Single leg (defender) > Shin whizzer > Limp arm counter
known_chains.note: Defender = being shot on. I whizzer, they limp arm, I counter their limp arm.

known_chains.sequence: Single leg (attacker) > Iranian > Grounded 50/50
Overhook > Ankle pick takedown
Underhook > Ankle pick > Single leg > Running the pipe

corrections_learned.lesson: Single under pass is from supine guard, not seated guard
Don't turn every spoken clause into a folder level
Final outcome positions (side-control, mount) as leaf folders usually unnecessary
Front headlock defender: defence = defensive escape (wrist control spin out). offence = attacking escape (knee-tap fireman).
Shin whizzer limp arm: 'I shoot, they whizzer, I limp arm' = attacker/offence. 'They shoot, I whizzer, they limp arm, I counter' = defender/defence.
Same technique can appear under both perspectives. Perspective = whose action it is.

suggestion_rules.high_confidence: Suggest one best folder. Use when sequence clearly maps to an existing chain pattern or a well-known position/heading/technique.
suggestion_rules.medium_confidence: Suggest 2-3 likely folders. Use when the position is clear but the chain or perspective could go multiple ways.
suggestion_rules.low_confidence: Report uncertainty. Ask Aaron. Use when the position is ambiguous (e.g. 'arm drag' without knowing which hand-fighting position).
suggestion_rules.never_guess_position: If the starting position is unclear, always ask.
suggestion_rules.never_over_nest: Collapse narrative mid-steps. Keep: sub-position, approach, finish.
